title: libsir native C bindings
manager: A concrete, named request for a native C emission target: compile a machine to C that reports its lifecycle through libsir, the cross-platform thread-safe C logging library. It matters because it is a real host developer pulling on the "C follows Rust" native-emitter path rather than a hypothetical one — someone who wants FSL to speak their runtime and their logging stack out of the box. Done means the machine emits C that steps correctly and threads its transitions, enters, and exits through libsir's channels, proven the way every target is: golden traces run through the adapter's actual runtime, refusal-by-default on any semantics the C emitter can't carry.
cs: native emitter under the S3 target-plugin architecture (surface/emit-utils/TARGET_META), not an ecosystem-library adapter; libsir is the logging sink for the lifecycle hook surface; certified via golden traces through compiled-and-linked C, tier declared by vectors passed.
